essence: Designs the architecture for AI‑driven CNC ecosystems, integrating machine learning, robotics, automation, and real‑time optimisation into unified autonomous manufacturing systems.

identity.title: CNC AI Systems Architect
identity.summary: Creates the high‑level architecture for autonomous CNC manufacturing, combining AI models, robotics, sensors, data pipelines, and control systems into a cohesive intelligent production environment.

salary.notes: Architects in aerospace, defence, robotics, or fully autonomous smart‑factory environments can exceed £220k-£300k+.

qualifications: Experience in AI engineering, robotics, or advanced manufacturing
Deep understanding of CNC machining, automation, and real‑time control
Knowledge of ML systems, reinforcement learning, and optimisation algorithms
Ability to design large‑scale autonomous manufacturing architectures

lifestyle.description: CNC AI systems architects work in aerospace, robotics, defence, and advanced manufacturing. They design the architecture for autonomous CNC ecosystems, integrating AI, robotics, sensors, and data systems. The role suits individuals who enjoy high‑level systems thinking, AI, and next‑generation manufacturing design.

habits: Integrates robotics, sensors, and real‑time optimisation
Defines data pipelines, control systems, and ML deployment
Collaborates with AI engineers, roboticists, and manufacturing leaders
Ensures scalability, reliability, and autonomy across the ecosystem
